NCT01575834.name: FRAME
NCT01575834.group: Postmenopausal women with osteoporosis, romosozumab 210 mg SC QM vs placebo (12 mo blinded)

allOutcomes.title: New vertebral fracture at 12 months (primary, RR)

NCT01575834.snippet: Source: Cosman F et al. N Engl J Med 2016;375:1532-1543 (FRAME).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 7,180 postmenopausal women aged 55-90 with T-score <=-2.5 at total hip or femoral neck randomized 1:1 double-blind to romosozumab 210 mg SC monthly (n=3,589) or matching placebo (n=3,591) for 12 months, then open-label denosumab in both arms.

evidence.label: Primary - New Vertebral Fracture at 12 Months
evidence.text: Incidence of new vertebral fracture at 12 months: romosozumab 16/3,321 (0.5%) vs placebo 59/3,322 (1.8%); relative risk 0.27 (95% CI 0.16 to 0.47; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Clinical Fracture and BMD
evidence.text: Clinical fracture at 12 months: romosozumab 58 vs placebo 90; HR 0.64 (95% CI 0.46-0.89). BMD increase at lumbar spine +13.3% vs +0% (between-group difference +12.7%).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matching placebo SC injections monthly. D3 LOW - 96% completion. D4 LOW - vertebral fractures centrally adjudicated by blinded radiologists. D5 LOW - SAP pre-specified.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 55 Years; max age 90 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~- Postmenopausal women with osteoporosis, defined as low bone mineral density (BMD T-score at the total hip or femoral neck of ≤ -2.50)~Exclusion Criteria:~* BMD T-score of ≤ -3.50 at the total hip or femoral neck~* History of hip fracture~* Any severe or more than 2 moderate vertebral fractures, as assessed by the central imaging based on lateral spine x-rays~* Use of agents affecting bone metabolism~* History of metabolic or bone disease (except osteoporosis)~* Vitamin D ...

NCT01631214.name: ARCH
NCT01631214.group: Postmenopausal women with osteoporosis and fragility fracture, romosozumab 12 mo then alendronate vs alendronate throughout (24 mo)
NCT01631214.snippet: Source: Saag KG et al. N Engl J Med 2017;377:1417-1427 (ARCH).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 4,093 postmenopausal women with osteoporosis and prior fragility fracture randomized 1:1 double-blind to romosozumab 210 mg SC QM for 12 months then alendronate 70 mg weekly (n=2,046) vs alendronate throughout 24 months (n=2,047).

evidence.label: Primary - New Vertebral Fracture through 24 Months
evidence.text: Cumulative incidence of new vertebral fracture through 24 months: romosozumab-to-alendronate 127/1,825 (6.2%) vs alendronate-only 243/1,834 (11.9%); relative risk 0.52 (95% CI 0.40 to 0.66; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Clinical and Hip Fracture
evidence.text: Clinical fracture through 24 months: 198 vs 266; HR 0.73 (95% CI 0.61-0.88). Hip fracture: 41 vs 66; HR 0.62 (0.42-0.92). Cardiovascular SAEs numerically higher in romosozumab arm (2.5% vs 1.9%) - FDA boxed warning issued.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by age and prior vertebral fracture. D2 LOW - double-blind + double-dummy (both oral weekly and SC monthly). D3 LOW - 89% completion. D4 LOW - central blinded radiographic adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 55 Years; max age 90 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~Postmenopausal women who meet at least one of the following bone mineral density (BMD) and fracture criteria:~* BMD T-score at the total hip or femoral neck of ≤ -2.50 and EITHER:~  * at least 1 moderate (semiquantitative grade [SQ]2) or severe (SQ3) vertebral fracture OR~ * at least 2 mild (SQ1) vertebral fractures OR~* BMD T-score at the total hip or femoral neck of ≤ -2.00 and EITHER:~  * at least 2 moderate (SQ2) or severe (SQ3) vertebral fractures OR~ * a fracture of ...

NCT02186171.name: BRIDGE
NCT02186171.group: Men with osteoporosis, romosozumab 210 mg SC QM vs placebo (12 mo blinded)

allOutcomes.title: BMD change lumbar spine at 12 months (%)

NCT02186171.snippet: Source: Lewiecki EM et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2018;103:3183-3193 (BRIDGE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 245 men aged 55-90 with osteoporosis (T-score <=-2.5 or <=-1.5 with prior fragility fracture) randomized 2:1 double-blind to romosozumab 210 mg SC monthly (n=163) or matching placebo (n=82) for 12 months.

evidence.label: Primary - Lumbar-Spine BMD at 12 Months
evidence.text: Mean percentage change in lumbar spine BMD at 12 months: romosozumab +12.1% vs placebo +1.2%; between-group difference +10.9% (95% CI +9.4 to +12.4; P<0.001). New vertebral fracture 1/163 (0.6%) vs 8/82 (9.8%); RR 0.06 (95% CI 0.01-0.44).

evidence.label: Secondary - Total Hip BMD
evidence.text: Total hip BMD change at 12 months: romosozumab +2.5% vs placebo -0.5%; between-group difference +2.9% (95% CI +2.2 to +3.6; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central stratified randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matching placebo. D3 LOW - 86% completion. D4 LOW - central DXA QA and blinded radiographic adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 55 Years; max age 90 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Must be ambulatory male subjects ≥ 55 years to ≤ 90 years of age~* Must have a BMD T score ≤ -2.50 at the spine or hip, or BMD T score ≤ -1.50 at the spine or hip and a history of fragility nonvertebral fracture or vertebral fracture.~Exclusion Criteria:~* A BMD T score ≤ -3.50 at the hip,~* History of hip fracture~* Severe metabolic bone diseases~* Significant laboratory abnormalities~* Recent treatment with agents affecting bone metabolism"

title: RapidMeta Endocrinology | Romosozumab in Postmenopausal Osteoporosis v1.0
